text: Mean age_years is different in patients with objective_response=1 vs objective_response=0; specifically responders are younger.
code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.objective_response==1,'age_years'], df.loc[df.objective_response==0,'age_years'], equal_var=False)
summary: Mean age in responders=65.01y vs non-responders=64.98y; t-test p=0.82.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative log-odds coefficient).
code: logit('objective_response ~ ecog_ps', data=df).fit()
summary: Logistic regression: log-odds per unit ecog_ps = -0.285 (p=9.53e-73); response rate by ECOG: {0: '0.282', 1: '0.228', 2: '0.182'}

text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with mcrpc=0.
code: chi2_contingency on mcrpc x objective_response
summary: Response rate mcrpc=1: 0.154; mcrpc=0: 0.346; chi2 p=0.

text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with visceral_mets=0.
code: chi2_contingency on visceral_mets x objective_response
summary: Response rate visceral_mets=1: 0.240; =0: 0.240; chi2 p=0.871.

text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative log-odds coefficient on log PSA).

summary: Logistic regression on log(1+PSA): beta=-0.304, p=2.57e-240.

text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_olaparib differs between brca2_mutation=1 and brca2_mutation=0 patients; specifically the benefit of treatment_olaparib=1 vs =0 is larger when brca2_mutation=1 than when brca2_mutation=0.
code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_olaparib * brca2_mutation + ecog_ps + visceral_mets + np.log1p(psa_ng_ml) + gleason_score + albumin_g_dl + np.log(ldh_u_l) + np.log(alkaline_phosphatase_u_l) + hemoglobin_g_dl + np.log(nlr) + np.log1p(crp_mg_l) + weight_loss_pct_6mo + age_years + mcrpc', data=df).fit()
summary: Adjusted interaction beta(treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation)=-0.282, p=0.0556. Tx benefit (treatment_olaparib=1 minus =0): when brca2_mutation=1 = -0.032 (n_treated=529); when brca2_mutation=0 = 0.003 (n_treated=4569).

text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab differs between msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients; specifically the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab=1 vs =0 is larger when msi_high=1 than when msi_high=0.
code: logit('objective_response ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * msi_high + ecog_ps + visceral_mets + np.log1p(psa_ng_ml) + gleason_score + albumin_g_dl + np.log(ldh_u_l) + np.log(alkaline_phosphatase_u_l) + hemoglobin_g_dl + np.log(nlr) + np.log1p(crp_mg_l) + weight_loss_pct_6mo + age_years + mcrpc', data=df).fit()

text: There exist treatment-by-feature interactions on objective_response such that the magnitude of treatment effect differs across at least one binary clinical/biomarker feature for at least one of the six treatments (systematic interaction screen).
code: for each treatment in tx and feature in binary_feats: logit('objective_response ~ treatment * feature + prognostic_covariates', data=df).fit()
summary: Top significant treatment x feature interactions (p<0.05): treatment_enzalutamide x mcrpc (beta=-2.01, p=0; tx benefit if mcrpc=1: 0.01 vs =0: 0.44); treatment_enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive (beta=-1.33, p=5.1e-102; tx benefit if ar_v7_positive=1: 0.01 vs =0: 0.25); treatment_enzalutamide x brca2_mutation (beta=-1.21, p=7.5e-45; tx benefit if brca2_mutation=1: 0.01 vs =0: 0.22); treatment_enzalutamide x msi_high (beta=-1.37, p=5.4e-21; tx benefit if msi_high=1: -0.02 vs =0: 0.21); treatment_lu177_psma x visceral_mets (beta=-0.22, p=0.004; tx benefit if visceral_mets=1: -0.02 vs =0: 0.01); treatment_abiraterone x brca2_mutation (beta=0.19, p=0.032; tx benefit if brca2_mutation=1: 0.02 vs =0: -0.01)

text: Treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response in patients with psma_high=1 but not in patients with psma_high=0; the treatment effect is concentrated in the psma_high=1 subgroup.

text: Treatment_olaparib increases objective_response in patients with brca2_mutation=1 but not in patients with brca2_mutation=0; the treatment effect is concentrated in the brca2_mutation=1 subgroup.

text: Treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response in patients with msi_high=1 but not in patients with msi_high=0; the treatment effect is concentrated in the msi_high=1 subgroup.

text: Treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response in patients with ar_v7_positive=0 but not in patients with ar_v7_positive=1; the treatment effect is suppressed in AR-V7-positive patients.

text: There exist significant treatment-by-continuous-feature interactions: the magnitude of treatment effect on objective_response depends on continuous patient features (ECOG, age, albumin, hemoglobin) for at least one treatment.

text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib increases objective_response specifically in patients with brca2_mutation=1; the complement subgroup brca2_mutation=0 shows no benefit.

text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response specifically in patients with msi_high=1; the complement subgroup msi_high=0 shows no benefit.

text: FINAL: treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response specifically in patients with psma_high=1; the complement subgroup psma_high=0 shows no benefit.
summary: Subgroup [psma_high==1]: response rate treatment_lu177_psma=1: 0.238 (n=4486), =0: 0.239 (n=25476); diff=-0.001; chi2 p=0.883. Complement: treatment_lu177_psma=1: 0.25016567263088135, =0: 0.24130434782608695.

text: FINAL: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response only in patients with ar_v7_positive=0; the AR-V7-positive subgroup shows no/diminished benefit because AR-V7 positivity suppresses response to AR-targeting therapy.
summary: Subgroup [ar_v7_positive==0]: response rate treatment_enzalutamide=1: 0.411 (n=16057), =0: 0.160 (n=23905); diff=0.251; chi2 p=0. Complement: treatment_enzalutamide=1: 0.16496640955461558, =0: 0.1565044027247051.

text: FINAL: treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response only in patients with ar_v7_positive=0; AR-V7 positivity suppresses the abiraterone benefit.
summary: Subgroup [ar_v7_positive==0]: response rate treatment_abiraterone=1: 0.257 (n=11968), =0: 0.262 (n=27994); diff=-0.005; chi2 p=0.308. Complement: treatment_abiraterone=1: 0.16341382732385049, =0: 0.15837491090520314.

text: REFINED: treatment_enzalutamide markedly increases objective_response specifically in the joint subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in patients with any of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, or msi_high=1, the benefit is essentially absent.
code: stratified chi2 on treatment_enzalutamide x objective_response within mcrpc==0 & ar_v7_positive==0 & brca2_mutation==0 & msi_high==0
summary: In joint favorable subgroup (n=15681): enz=1 rate=0.798 (n=6325), enz=0 rate=0.172 (n=9356), diff=0.626, chi2 p=0.

text: In the complement subgroup (mcrpc=1 OR ar_v7_positive=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 OR msi_high=1), treatment_enzalutamide does NOT meaningfully increase objective_response.
code: stratified chi2 on treatment_enzalutamide x objective_response in complement of joint favorable subgroup

text: Relaxing only the mcrpc=0 condition (i.e. mcrpc=1 with ar_v7=0, brca2=0, msi_high=0) abolishes the enzalutamide benefit.
summary: Subgroup with only mcrpc=1 unfavorable (n=19163): enz=1 rate=0.159 (n=7677), enz=0 rate=0.152 (n=11486), diff=0.007, p=0.17.

text: Relaxing only the ar_v7_positive=0 condition abolishes the enzalutamide benefit.
summary: Subgroup with only ar_v7_positive=1 unfavorable (n=3940): enz=1 rate=0.182 (n=1573), enz=0 rate=0.161 (n=2367), diff=0.021, p=0.0859.

text: Relaxing only the brca2_mutation=0 condition abolishes the enzalutamide benefit.
summary: Subgroup with only brca2_mutation=1 unfavorable (n=1775): enz=1 rate=0.152 (n=710), enz=0 rate=0.148 (n=1065), diff=0.004, p=0.881.

text: Relaxing only the msi_high=0 condition abolishes the enzalutamide benefit.
summary: Subgroup with only msi_high=1 unfavorable (n=494): enz=1 rate=0.185 (n=189), enz=0 rate=0.210 (n=305), diff=-0.025, p=0.583.

text: REFINED: treatment_lu177_psma yields a modest increase in objective_response specifically in patients with visceral_mets=0; in patients with visceral_mets=1 the apparent effect is negative or null.
code: stratified chi2 on treatment_lu177_psma x objective_response within visceral_mets subgroups

text: Treatment_lu177_psma benefit on objective_response does NOT differ between psma_high=1 and psma_high=0 (no significant interaction in this dataset).
code: stratified chi2 on treatment_lu177_psma x objective_response within psma_high subgroups

text: REFINED: treatment_pembrolizumab yields a marginal, small increase in objective_response in patients with visceral_mets=0; the effect is null/negative in patients with visceral_mets=1.
code: stratified chi2 on treatment_pembrolizumab x objective_response within visceral_mets subgroups
summary: visceral_mets=0 (n=40027): pembro=1 rate=0.246, =0 rate=0.240, diff=0.006, p=0.575. visceral_mets=1 (n=9973): pembro=1 rate=0.211, =0 rate=0.241, diff=-0.031, p=0.135.

text: In patients with msi_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT show a clear increase in objective_response (no detectable benefit beyond background; possibly underpowered with small subgroup).
code: stratified chi2 on treatment_pembrolizumab x objective_response within msi_high==1

text: REFINED: contrary to the textbook expectation that PARP inhibitors selectively benefit BRCA2-mutated patients, in this dataset treatment_olaparib does NOT increase objective_response in brca2_mutation=1 patients (the apparent within-subgroup effect is null or slightly negative).
code: stratified chi2 on treatment_olaparib x objective_response within brca2_mutation==1
summary: brca2_mutation=1 (n=4996): olaparib=1 rate=0.121, =0 rate=0.153, diff=-0.032, p=0.0581.

text: REFINED: treatment_abiraterone shows a small positive increase in objective_response specifically in brca2_mutation=1 patients; in brca2_mutation=0 patients the effect is essentially null.
code: stratified chi2 on treatment_abiraterone x objective_response within brca2_mutation==1

text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response: enzalutamide increases objective_response by ~+0.63 in absolute terms (rate ~0.80 vs ~0.17) in the joint subgroup { mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0 }; in any patient with mcrpc=1 OR ar_v7_positive=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 OR msi_high=1 the benefit is ~0 (no effect). Each of these four features individually appears to suppress the enzalutamide effect.
code: joint subgroup chi2 + multivariable logistic regression with treatment_enzalutamide x {mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high} interactions
summary: Joint favorable subgroup n=15681: enz benefit = +0.626 (p<1e-300). Complement n=34319: enz benefit = +0.008 (p=0.06).

text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response: lu177_psma yields a small positive effect on objective_response specifically in patients with visceral_mets=0 (~+0.01 absolute), and a null/negative effect in visceral_mets=1; psma_high status does NOT modify the effect in this dataset (contrary to standard clinical expectation).
code: stratified chi2 + interaction logistic regression
summary: visceral_mets=0 (n=40027): lu177 benefit = +0.010, p<0.05. visceral_mets=1 (n=9973): lu177 benefit = -0.024.

text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response: the effect is small overall and at most marginal in visceral_mets=0; the textbook MSI-high responder subgroup is not detectable here (small msi_high subgroup, n=1528 with only ~79 pembro recipients).
code: stratified chi2 within visceral_mets and msi_high subgroups
summary: visceral_mets=0 (n=40027): pembro benefit ≈ +0.006. visceral_mets=1 (n=9973): pembro benefit ≈ -0.031. MSI-high (n=1528): pembro benefit not significant.

text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_olaparib on objective_response: olaparib does NOT show a useful response benefit in any subgroup we tested in this dataset; in particular it does not appear to selectively benefit brca2_mutation=1 patients (their within-subgroup benefit is null/slightly negative).
code: stratified chi2 + interaction logistic regression
summary: Overall olaparib benefit ≈ -0.001 (NS). BRCA2+ subgroup (n=4996): olaparib benefit = -0.032 (NS).

text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_abiraterone on objective_response: abiraterone shows essentially no overall effect, with a small (marginally significant) positive effect specifically in brca2_mutation=1 patients.
code: stratified chi2 + interaction logistic regression
summary: Overall abiraterone benefit ≈ 0 (NS). BRCA2+ subgroup (n=4996): abi benefit ~ +0.021.

text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_docetaxel on objective_response: docetaxel does not show a meaningful main effect or any heterogeneity by the binary biomarkers tested in this cohort.
code: main-effect chi2 + treatment x feature interaction screen
summary: treatment_docetaxel main effect: rate diff ~ +0.000 (p>0.5). No significant treatment_docetaxel x feature interaction (all p>0.2).
